programme.title: Quo Vadis TV
programme.desc: Quo Vadis TV is committed to sharing the Biblical message in a way that makes Christian living practical, realistic, and attainable. Our theme is summarized the question: "where are you going?" Our Aim with this inquiry is to help people find orientation and find the path back to God.

programme.title: Hope and Health With Mathew Embry
programme.desc: Retired anesthesiologist Bill Code about living with multiple sclerosis for nearly three decades. Diagnosed in 1996, Bill shares how his medical background and personal experience shaped his long-term approach to managing MS. The conversation explores hyperbaric oxygen therapy, omega-3 supplementation, the microvascular theory of MS, and the role of blood flow and cellular energy in neurological health.

programme.title: Hope and Health With Mathew Embry
programme.desc: Angie Gensler is a mother and personal trainer who was diagnosed with multiple sclerosis in 2004. In this episode, Mathew Embry speaks with Angie about her diagnosis and why she ultimately chose not to pursue drug treatments. Instead, she focused on lifestyle changes including diet, exercise, and daily discipline. Along the way she faced several major challenges, all while continuing to pursue optimal health and eventually stepping onto the competitive bodybuilding stage in her 50s.

programme.title: Shotgun
programme.desc: A deputy sheriff (Sterling Hayden) seeks justice with a woman (Yvonne De Carlo), a bounty hunter (Zachary Scott) and a sawed-off shotgun.

programme.title: Hands Across the Rockies
programme.desc: Wild Bill Hickok and his sidekick Cannonball meet a woman who is scheduled to marry the murderer they are trailing.

programme.title: Roughshod
programme.desc: Rancher Clay (Robert Sterling) and his brother, Steve (Claude Jarman Jr.), head out across the Sonora mountain pass, followed by Lednov (John Ireland), an ex-con seeking revenge on Clay for putting him behind bars. Clay and Steve unexpectedly cross paths with a group of dance hall girls -- including Mary (Gloria Grahame), Marcia (Martha Hyer) and Helen (Myrna Dell) -- whose stagecoach has broken down, and help them get to the nearest ranch, where Lednov unfortunately catches up to Clay.

programme.title: Man in the Saddle
programme.desc: A wounded rancher (Randolph Scott) hides with a schoolmarm (Ellen Drew) after his ex-girlfriend's husband (Alexander Knox) runs him out of town.

programme.title: Renegades
programme.desc: Hannah Brockway has a perfect fiancé in Dr. Sam Martin. Sam, who is respected by Hannah's family, is a noble man who cares for her and can make a good living in their frontier town. Yet Hannah craves adventure and finds it when she meets dashing Ben Dembrow, the youngest of a notorious crime family. Together Ben and Hannah set off to forge a new life, but prejudice and strangling family ties soon bring trouble to these newlyweds.

programme.title: Life With Father
programme.desc: In late 19th-century New York City, stockbroker Clarence Day (William Powell) strives to maintain order in his bustling household. Despite his attempts to be the chief authority over his four sons, his wife, Vinnie (Irene Dunne), is the one who truly keeps order in their home, much to Clarence's chagrin. To gain more respect, Clarence reluctantly considers making changes that will benefit everyone, including his own baptism. This slice-of-life comedy was based on a popular Broadway play.

programme.title: Day of the Badman
programme.desc: Judge Jim Scott (Fred MacMurray) wants to sentence a killer to die, but the outlaw's family members intend otherwise. All-powerful patriarch Charlie Hayes (Robert Middleton) and his intimidating kinfolk are confident they can use violence to get their doomed relative's sentence commuted into something less severe. Although Sheriff Barney Wiley (John Ericson) wilts under the family's strong-arm tactics, Scott remains determined to see justice done at the end of a rope.

programme.title: Hobson's Choice
programme.desc: Henry Hobson (Charles Laughton), a British widower, is the overbearing owner of a shoe shop. His three daughters -- Alice, Vicky and Maggie (Brenda De Banzie) -- work for him and all are eager to get out from under his thumb. When the headstrong Maggie announces she intends to marry Henry's best employee, Will (John Mills), father and daughter engage in an intense showdown. As Maggie works on launching a competing business, she also helps her sisters free themselves of their domineering father.

programme.title: Desert Vigilante
programme.desc: The Durango Kid (Charles Starrett) bags Mexican-border silver smugglers for Uncle Sam.

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Holmes links a murder in a quiet English house to a Chicago gangster.

programme.title: 2026 Proof Silver Eagle: America's 250th Anniversary Release
programme.desc: Join Mike Mezack for a historic release, the first-ever 1776-2026 dual-date Proof Silver Eagle with Liberty Bell privy mark, struck in .999 silver for America's 250th Anniversary; a one-year-only issue collectors won't want to miss.

programme.title: The Gay Cavalier
programme.desc: The Cisco Kid (Gilbert Roland) and Pancho (Martin Garralaga) try to help an unhappy bride.

programme.title: Through the Bible With Les Feldick
programme.desc: Les Feldick, an Oklahoma farmer and rancher with a special gift for teaching the Bible; his simple and nondenominational approach on the program "Through the Bible with Les Feldick" has reached millions around the world with the Gospel of Grace.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Earp and Bat find their careers in jeopardy when they arrest the owners of two competing stagecoach companies whose hired armies threaten to start a war in the streets.

programme.title: The 7th Voyage of Sinbad
programme.desc: Sailing to Baghdad after a narrow escape from the monster island of Colossa, the wedding plans of legendary hero Sinbad and Princess Parisa are spoiled by the scheming sorcerer Sokurah. In return for his previous help with the cyclops on Colossa, Sokurah demands that Sinbad retrieve a lamp he lost on the island. When Sinbad refuses, the conjurer shrinks Parisa, forcing the sailor and his crew back to the high seas in order to save her.

programme.title: Brannigan
programme.desc: A Chicago police detective (John Wayne) hunts a mobster in London with a policewoman escort (Judy Geeson) from Scotland Yard.

programme.title: A Month in the Country

programme.title: The Girl from San Lorenzo
programme.desc: Cisco and Pancho set out to clear their names in a series of stage robberies committed by two thugs who are impersonating them.

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Holmes is called in when a top secret document is stolen from the British Foreign Office.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Earp and Bat work together to help Bat win an election for county sheriff to defeat Jerry Lanphere, a crook also running for the position; Sen. Drew tells Bat he must end his friendship with Earp to gain the people's favor.

programme.desc: A practical-joking cowboy (Buck Jones) gets serious with outlaws.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Earp has 24 hours to find a killer after a judge sentences an innocent man to hang, but the man refuses to speak in his own defense, leaving Earp with few clues to follow.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Bounty hunters stir up trouble in Dodge City by trying to collect rewards on reformed outlaws until Earp devises a plan to turn the bounty hunters against each other.

programme.title: The 7th Voyage of Sinbad
programme.desc: Sinbad the sailor (Kerwin Mathews) needs the egg of a giant two-headed bird of prey to restore a shrunken princess (Kathryn Grant).

programme.title: Action in the North Atlantic
programme.desc: The captain (Raymond Massey), mates (Humphrey Bogart, Alan Hale) and crew of a Liberty Ship dodge U-boats, dive bombers and foul weather to reach port.

programme.title: Brannigan
programme.desc: A Chicago police detective (John Wayne) hunts a mobster in London with a policewoman escort (Judy Geeson) from Scotland Yard.

programme.title: The Ride to Hangman's Tree
programme.desc: Three Old West outlaws (Jack Lord, James Farentino, Don Galloway) cross paths in California during Wells Fargo stagecoach holdups.

programme.title: King of the Wild Horses
programme.desc: A mustang stallion runs swiftly to the rescue when the boy who befriended him is endangered.

programme.title: The Green Berets
programme.desc: A cynical reporter (David Janssen) who is opposed to the Vietnam War is sent to cover the conflict and assigned to tag along with a group of Green Berets. Led by the tough-as-nails Col. Mike Kirby (John Wayne), the team is given a top-secret mission to sneak behind enemy lines and kidnap an important Viet Cong commander. Along the way, the reporter learns to respect why America is involved in the war and helps to save the life of a war orphan whose life has been destroyed by the conflict.

programme.title: Battle of the Bulge
programme.desc: American Lt. Col. Dan Kiley (Henry Fonda), a military intelligence whiz, discovers that the Nazis are planning to attack Allied forces near Belgium. Certain that the exhausted enemy can't muster much force, Gen. Joe Grey (Robert Ryan) isn't convinced by Kiley's findings, and his men pay the price when the German tanks begin their offensive. In the heat of this key World War II battle, Kiley must come up with a plan when it becomes clear that the Nazis are trying to steal fuel from the Allies.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: A headstrong teenager named Jimmy Craig arrives in Dodge City and wants to become one of Earp's deputies, demonstrating his impeccable skill with a gun, but his dream contrasts sharply with his irrational obsession to duel Ed Beatty.

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Holmes and Watson uncover a plot to marry a beautiful music teacher for her inheritance.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Earp takes Bat's brother Ed under his wing as a new deputy, but while Ed believes outlaws can be reasoned with, Bat knows better; two businessmen plan to kill Ed and frame Bat for the crime to secure ownership of a saloon.

programme.title: King of the Bandits
programme.desc: The Cisco Kid and Pancho track an outlaw responsible for a series of stagecoach robberies and masquerading as the Kid.

programme.title: Betrayed
programme.desc: When the Dutch resistance brings in Carla Van Oven (Lana Turner) to spy on the Nazis, Col. Pieter Deventer (Clark Gable) initially suspects she could be a double agent. Van Oven eventually convinces Deventer of her character, and when she is sent into occupied territory, she joins up with a rebel leader known as "The Scarf" (Victor Mature). As the resistance endures severe losses and their missions fail, Deventer starts to wonder whose side Van Oven is fighting on.

programme.title: Paid Programming: Carshield -- Cars Have Changed
Action in the North Atlantic
programme.desc: Merchant Marine sailors Joe Rossi (Humphrey Bogart) and Steve Jarvis (Raymond Massey) are charged with getting a supply vessel to Russian allies as part of a sea convoy. When the group of ships comes under attack from a German U-boat, Rossi and Jarvis navigate through dangerous waters to evade Nazi naval forces. Though their mission across the Atlantic is extremely treacherous, they are motivated by the opportunity to strike back at the Germans, who sank one of their earlier ships.

programme.title: Above and Beyond
programme.desc: In this moving 1953 drama from director Melvin Frank, Col. Paul Tibbets, commander of the 509th Composite Group during World War II, struggles to come to grips with his peculiar place in human history: He's the first man to ever drop an atomic bomb. While his wife, Lucy, has stayed loyal to him, the aftermath of causing such destruction leaves Paul questioning his humanity and the mission that may have forever diminished it.

programme.title: 36 Hours
programme.desc: In occupied Germany in 1949, American military officer Jefferson Pike (James Garner) wakes up in a U.S. Army hospital with no memory of the previous five years. A kindly military psychiatrist (Rod Taylor) and his coolly detached nurse (Eva Marie Saint) draw out Pike's last clear memories, which are of General Eisenhower's final briefing for the Normandy invasion in June 1944. Soon, Pike realizes that his doctor and nurse are not who they claim to be, and that he is in great danger.

programme.title: The Green Berets
programme.desc: A cynical reporter (David Janssen) who is opposed to the Vietnam War is sent to cover the conflict and assigned to tag along with a group of Green Berets. programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: A headstrong teenager named Jimmy Craig arrives in Dodge City and wants to become one of Earp's deputies, demonstrating his impeccable skill with a gun, but his dream contrasts sharply with his irrational obsession to duel Ed Beatty.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Earp takes Bat's brother Ed under his wing as a new deputy, but while Ed believes outlaws can be reasoned with, Bat knows better; two businessmen plan to kill Ed and frame Bat for the crime to secure ownership of a saloon.

programme.title: Scalplock
programme.desc: A frontier gambler (Dale Robertson) wins a railroad in a card game and has to keep it going as people try to take it away from him.

programme.title: Whispering Smith
programme.desc: Luke Whispering Smith (Alan Ladd) is a mild-mannered policeman tasked with stopping a gang of train robbers, led by Barney Rebstock (Donald Crisp). Luke is faced with a conflict of interest when his good friend, Murray Sinclair (Robert Preston), loses his railroad job and, in an act of defiance, joins Rebstock's gang of outlaws. To make things even more complicated, Luke's friendship with Murray's ex-wife, Marian (Brenda Marshall), is becoming romantic.

programme.title: Man of the West
programme.desc: Ex-outlaw Link Jones (Gary Cooper) is aboard a train when it is robbed by bandits. When he tries to intervene, he's knocked out and left in the middle of nowhere with saloon singer Billie (Julie London), whom he hoped to hire as a schoolteacher in his town, and Sam (Arthur O'Connell), a con man. Link brings the three to his nearby former home, now the hideout for the bandits, who are led by his uncle (Lee J. Cobb) -- and to save his new friends, he must rejoin the old gang for one last holdup.

programme.title: The Last Challenge
programme.desc: Hotshot gunfighter Lot McGuire intends to make a name for himself by out-duelling legendary bandit-turned-lawman Marshal Dan Blaine. Before any bullets fly, however, Lot and his intended enemy strike up an unlikely friendship, and Blaine tries to convince the youngster to call off the shootout. Unfortunately, Lot can't be talked out of his quest for glory, and so he guns down a few hostile locals, leaving Blaine no choice but to finally accept the offer for a showdown.

programme.title: Beach Party
programme.desc: Dolores (Annette Funicello) and Frankie (Frankie Avalon), attractive young surfers in Southern California, are just out for a good time. Their relaxed lifestyle fascinates Professor Sutwell (Bob Cummings), an anthropologist who decides to study the social lives of teens. A series of beach-themed films would follow the blueprint set by this one, which hinges on romantic rivalries, rugged motorcyclists, an attempted crime and plenty of handsome people.

programme.title: Bikini Beach
programme.desc: Dee Dee (Annette Funicello) and Frankie (Frankie Avalon) are primed to hit the beach for some fun in the sun. The only thing standing in the way of their good times is curmudgeonly developer Harvey Huntington Honeywagon (Keenan Wynn), who is intent on turning their teenage hotspot into a grazing ground for seniors. If that weren't bad enough, British rock 'n' roll singer Potato Bug (also Avalon) shows up to spark a summer romance with Frankie's main squeeze -- Dee Dee.

programme.title: Whispering Smith
programme.desc: Luke Whispering Smith (Alan Ladd) is a mild-mannered policeman tasked with stopping a gang of train robbers, led by Barney Rebstock (Donald Crisp). Luke is faced with a conflict of interest when his good friend, Murray Sinclair (Robert Preston), loses his railroad job and, in an act of defiance, joins Rebstock's gang of outlaws. To make things even more complicated, Luke's friendship with Murray's ex-wife, Marian (Brenda Marshall), is becoming romantic.

programme.title: Man of the West
programme.desc: Ex-outlaw Link Jones (Gary Cooper) is aboard a train when it is robbed by bandits. Link brings the three to his nearby former home, now the hideout for the bandits, who are led by his uncle (Lee J. Cobb) -- and to save his new friends, he must rejoin the old gang for one last holdup.

programme.desc: Now that Gru has forsaken a life of crime to raise Margo, Agnes and Edith, he's trying to figure out how to provide for his new family. As he struggles with his responsibilities as a father, the Anti-Villain League, an organization dedicated to fighting evil, comes calling. The AVL sends Gru on a mission to capture the perpetrator of a spectacular heist, for who would be better than the world's greatest ex-villain to capture the individual who seeks to usurp his power.

programme.title: Minions
programme.desc: Evolving from single-celled yellow organisms at the dawn of time, Minions live to serve, but find themselves working for a continual series of unsuccessful masters, from T. Rex to Napoleon. Without a master to grovel for, the Minions fall into a deep depression. But one minion, Kevin, has a plan; accompanied by his pals Stuart and Bob, Kevin sets forth to find a new evil boss for his brethren to follow. Their search leads them to Scarlet Overkill, the world's first-ever super-villainess.

programme.desc: "Bloomberg Balance of Power" with host David Westin is a daily look at how politics is impacting global business decisions; top lawmakers, business executives and government leaders join to discuss doing business in today's volatile political climate.

programme.title: Live: Bloomberg Businessweek Daily
programme.desc: Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec bring reporting from the magazine that helps global leaders stay ahead, plus insight on the people, companies and trends shaping today's complex economy.

programme.title: Live: Bloomberg The Asia Trade
programme.desc: Bringing global investors everything you need to know as the trading day begins in Asia; Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping markets, from tech, to geopolitics, and the world's energy transition.

programme.title: Bloomberg Originals
programme.desc: "Bloomberg Originals" offers bold takes for curious minds on today's biggest topics; hosted by experts covering stories you haven't seen and viewpoints you haven't heard, you'll discover cinematic, data-led shows that investigate the intersection of business and culture; exploring every angle of climate change, technology, finance, sports and beyond, "Bloomberg Originals" is business as you've never seen it.

programme.title: The Circuit With Emily Chang
programme.desc: S05E02 Emily Chang meets 23andMe Institute Chief Executive Officer Anne Wojcicki for a discussion of the rise, fall and rebirth of the personal genetics company.

programme.title: Live: Insight With Haslinda Amin
programme.desc: In a volatile and unpredictable world, the show will be the definitive guide for the global news audience to get the complete picture on the biggest story of the day. With news making executives, officials and thought-leaders from the world of business, politics and academia we will explore a story through probing interviews and nuanced conversations. The hour of programming will also highlight the most relevant stories from India which is currently one of the hottest investment destinations, but also has compelling stories that play into the Bloomberg's priority issues such as Climate change, Diversity and Technological innovation.

programme.title: Live: Bloomberg Horizons Middle East & Africa
programme.desc: A spotlight on the fast paced growth and major deals happening across the Middle East and Africa; linking the market drivers from overnight in the US and across Asia into the region's trading day, with deep dive analysis of the top business, energy and geopolitical stories shaping the decisions of traders across the Middle East, Africa and beyond.

programme.title: Live: Bloomberg Daybreak: Europe
programme.desc: From London, essential morning viewing to stay ahead of the day's market trends with news stories from the U.S. and Asia in the lead-up to the opening of European markets.

programme.desc: "The Opening Trade" explores things to know as markets open across Europe; analysis that isn't available anywhere else, a break-down of the biggest stories of the day and top guests who have skin in the game.

programme.title: Leaders with Lacqua
programme.desc: What drives the world's most influential minds?; in Leaders with Francine Lacqua, Bloomberg's award-winning anchor sits down with the decision-makers shaping our future to explore the personal stories behind their public personas; discover what inspires them, how they navigate power and the lessons they offer on defining success. Insightful, cinematic and elegantly crafted, "Leaders" goes beyond the headlines to reveal the human side of leadership.

programme.title: The David Rubenstein Show: Peer to Peer Conversations
programme.desc: Financier and philanthropist David Rubenstein talks with his peers - leaders of some of America's biggest companies - in this interview show that aims to answer the question of what makes a great leader; Rubenstein uses his chats to explore successful leadership skills through the personal and professional choices made by the business world's most influential people; Rubenstein travels across the country to find out about the business leaders' stories and uncover information about their paths to success; the interview subjects include Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates, Berkshire Hathaway chairman Warren Buffett and Pepsi CEO Indra Nooyi.

programme.desc: Mishal Husain brings her signature blend of curiosity and tenacity to weekly conversations with world leaders, business titans and cultural icons, revealing who they really are and how they see the world changing around them.

programme.desc: Bringing together some of the world's most influential financial and economic thinkers, policymakers and business leaders to prepare a blueprint for the next stage of global growth.

programme.desc: A weekly program telling the stories of capitalism from around the world, David Westin in New York hosts.

programme.title: Bloomberg Chief Future Officer
programme.desc: Chief Financial Officers now play a critical role in shaping corporate strategy and positioning organizations; profiling AT&T CFO Pascal Desroches, who has partnered with John Stankey to restructure the company.

programme.title: Bloomberg Investigates
programme.desc: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission is supposed to root out graft. Instead, some of its members have been accused of perpetuating the very wrongdoing they were meant to stop.

programme.title: Bloomberg Wall Street Week
programme.desc: A weekly program telling the stories of capitalism from around the world, David Westin in New York hosts.

programme.title: Live: Bloomberg This Weekend
programme.desc: The news doesn't stop when markets close; hosts David Gura, Christina Ruffini and Lisa Mateo bring clarity, context and a bit of humor to the weekend's biggest headlines, LIVE from New York.

programme.title: The David Rubenstein Show: Peer to Peer Conversations
programme.desc: Financier and philanthropist David Rubenstein talks with his peers - leaders of some of America's biggest companies - in this interview show that aims to answer the question of what makes a great leader; Rubenstein uses his chats to explore successful leadership skills through the personal and professional choices made by the business world's most influential people; Rubenstein travels across the country to find out about the business leaders' stories and uncover information about their paths to success; the interview subjects include Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates, Berkshire Hathaway chairman Warren Buffett and Pepsi CEO Indra Nooyi.

programme.title: Leaders with Lacqua
programme.desc: What drives the world's most influential minds?; in Leaders with Francine Lacqua, Bloomberg's award-winning anchor sits down with the decision-makers shaping our future to explore the personal stories behind their public personas; discover what inspires them, how they navigate power and the lessons they offer on defining success. Insightful, cinematic and elegantly crafted, "Leaders" goes beyond the headlines to reveal the human side of leadership.

programme.title: The Circuit With Emily Chang
programme.desc: S05E02 Emily Chang meets 23andMe Institute Chief Executive Officer Anne Wojcicki for a discussion of the rise, fall and rebirth of the personal genetics company.

programme.title: Bloomberg Originals

programme.title: Bloomberg Wall Street Week

programme.title: Live: Bloomberg The Asia Trade
programme.desc: Bringing global investors everything you need to know as the trading day begins in Asia; Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping markets, from tech, to geopolitics, and the world's energy transition.

programme.title: Live: Bloomberg: The China Show
programme.desc: Your definitive source for news and analysis on the world's second-biggest economy; from politics and policy to tech and trends, Yvonne Man and David Ingles give global investors unique insight, delivering in-depth discussions with the newsmakers who matter.

programme.title: Live: Bloomberg Markets: Asia
programme.desc: In an effort to provide extensive coverage of worldwide financial markets, Bloomberg features several regional shows under the "Bloomberg Markets" banner, with each focusing on that geographic area's markets. This Asian-focused version is hosted by Shery Ahn, David Ingles, Haidi Lun, Rishaad Salamat and Angie Lau. They report the latest business news and speak with the likes of political and business leaders to help investors make investment decisions that build their portfolios.

programme.title: Live: Bloomberg Horizons Middle East & Africa
programme.desc: A spotlight on the fast paced growth and major deals happening across the Middle East and Africa; linking the market drivers from overnight in the US and across Asia into the region's trading day, with deep dive analysis of the top business, energy and geopolitical stories shaping the decisions of traders across the Middle East, Africa and beyond.

programme.title: Live: Bloomberg Daybreak: Europe
programme.desc: From London, essential morning viewing to stay ahead of the day's market trends with news stories from the U.S. and Asia in the lead-up to the opening of European markets.

programme.title: Live: Bloomberg The Opening Trade
programme.desc: "The Opening Trade" explores things to know as markets open across Europe; analysis that isn't available anywhere else, a break-down of the biggest stories of the day and top guests who have skin in the game.

programme.title: Live: The Pulse with Francine Lacqua
programme.desc: "The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics; based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

programme.title: Battle at Apache Pass
programme.desc: (1952) Western. Apache chief Cochise is trying to keep the peace with the cavalry unit based at Fort Buchanan, but he's undermined by a government advisor and a rival Apache chief.

programme.title: Deep Impact
programme.desc: (1998) Elijah Wood and Morgan Freeman star in the sci-fi disaster movie about impending meteorite-related disaster, attempts to destroy it, and possibly some cataclysmic havoc.

programme.title: Rescue Dawn
programme.desc: (2006) Werner Herzog's fact-based survival story stars Christian Bale as a US fighter pilot taken prisoner in Vietnam after being shot down during a top-secret mission.

programme.title: M3GAN
programme.desc: (2022) Creepy-doll horror with Allison Williams. A robotics engineer simultaneously invents an AI dolly and takes on the care of her orphaned niece. But can a cyborg really raise a kid?

programme.title: Corsage
programme.desc: (2022) Marie Kreutzer's tightly corseted biographical drama starring Vicky Krieps as a quietly defiant Empress Elisabeth of Austria as she turns 40 on Christmas Eve 1877. In German/subs.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: The Nevadan
programme.desc: (1950) Western with Randolph Scott and Dorothy Malone. A cunning US Marshal pretends to be a doofus to get a bank robber to take him to the hidden loot.

programme.title: The Caine Mutiny
programme.desc: (1954) Oscar-nominated Humphrey Bogart, in one of his finest roles, stars in Edward Dmytryk's gripping drama as Captain Philip Queeg, in charge of a US Navy minesweeper during the war.

programme.title: Santa Fe
programme.desc: (1951) Western starring Randolph Scott. Four brothers leave the defeated South to seek jobs as the US recovers from the Civil War. As one becomes a railwayman, the others turn to crime.

programme.title: Apache Drums
programme.desc: (1951) Stephen McNally stars as a gunslinger who gets slung out of town for gambling too well, but the town might just need him soon in Hugo Fregonese's visually arresting western.

programme.title: The Land Girls
programme.desc: (1997) Moving Second World War drama starring Catherine McCormack, Rachel Weisz and Anna Friel as young women who volunteer to be agricultural workers and fall for the farmer's son.

programme.title: Erin Brockovich
programme.desc: (2000) Fact-based legal drama. Julia Roberts won an Oscar, Golden Globe and BAFTA for her portrayal of a single mum making a huge impact at a legal firm despite having no formal training.

programme.title: Bombshell
programme.desc: (2019) Drama with Charlize Theron, Nicole Kidman and Margot Robbie. Three Fox News employees fight to expose the inappropriate behaviour of a media mogul (John Lithgow).

programme.title: Hear My Song
programme.desc: (1991) Unashamedly romantic comedy starring Ned Beatty as Josef Locke, a 1950s singer with a voice that makes women weep... in a good way. Co-starring Adrian Dunbar.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: Reap the Wild Wind
programme.desc: (1942) Period melodrama. John Wayne, Ray Milland and Paulette Goddard star in this tale of fierce rivalries engendered by love and seemingly deliberate shipwrecking in 19th-century Florida.

programme.title: Send Me No Flowers
programme.desc: (1964) Romcom starring Rock Hudson and Doris Day. Thinking he has just a few months to live, a man makes his wife suspect he's having an affair when he tries to find her a new partner.

programme.title: The Last Wagon
programme.desc: (1956) Western. Richard Widmark plays a man who murdered the killers of his wife and children and who becomes the unlikely saviour of a wagon train that's attacked by Apaches.

programme.title: Decision at Sundown
programme.desc: (1957) Western starring Randolph Scott and John Carroll. A man seeking revenge for his wife's death tracks his rival down in the sleepy town of Sundown...

programme.title: Taxi
programme.desc: (2004) All-action thriller. Queen Latifah stars as the driver of the fastest taxi in New York, who teams up with an inept cop (Jimmy Fallon) to catch a team of female bank-robbers.

programme.title: Nope
programme.desc: (2022) Western-infused sci-fi chiller with Daniel Kaluuya and Keke Palmer as cash-strapped horse trainers who try to supplement their income by filming alien goings-on at their ranch.

programme.title: Monolith
programme.desc: (2022) Sci-fi horror with Lily Sullivan as a disgraced journalist investigating mysterious manifestations of vision-inducing - possibly supernatural - black bricks. Matt Vesley directs.

programme.title: Significant Other
programme.desc: (2022) Eerie sci-fi horror-thriller with Maika Monroe and Jake Lacy. Extra-terrestrial forces derail a couple's romantic backpacking trip, but in a subtle, non-spaceship kind of a way.

programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: D-Day the Sixth of June
programme.desc: (1956) Wartime romance set against the background of the D-Day landings, starring Robert Taylor and Dana Wynter. A married American officer embarks on an affair with English nurse.

programme.title: The Longest Day
programme.desc: (1962) War drama. Darryl F Zanuck's classic Oscar-winning recreation of the Allied landings in Normandy on 6 June 1944, with an all-star British, American, German and French cast.

programme.title: Spies in Disguise
programme.desc: (2019) Animated spy comedy caper with the voice of Will Smith. The 'world's most awesome spy' gets framed and has to go undercover as a pigeon to prove his innocence.

programme.title: Sister Act
programme.desc: (1992) Comedy starring Whoopi Goldberg. A casino singer who witnesses a Mob murder goes into hiding at a convent, where she soon has the choir rocking. With Maggie Smith.

programme.title: Django Unchained Interview Special
programme.desc: Director Quentin Tarantino and stars Samuel L Jackson, Christoph Waltz, Jamie Foxx and Kerry Washington talk about making the western adventure set against a backdrop of slavery.

programme.title: Django Unchained
programme.desc: (2012) Quentin Tarantino's darkly comic, award-laden western with Jamie Foxx, Christopher Waltz, Leonardo DiCaprio and Samuel L Jackson. A freed slave searches for his wife... and revenge.

programme.title: The Entity
programme.desc: (1982) Sidney J Furie's seminal horror stars Barbara Hershey as a divorced mother of three who becomes the victim of a poltergeist that submits her to multiple violent and sexual assaults.

programme.title: The Mark of Zorro
programme.desc: (1940) Swashbuckling action-adventure starring Tyrone Power. An aristocrat dons his mask and cape to fight for the rights of the poor in 19th-century California. With Basil Rathbone.

programme.title: Bridge of Spies Interview Special
programme.desc: Director Steven Spielberg and stars Tom Hanks and Mark Rylance talk about making their Cold War espionage drama.

programme.title: The Andromeda Strain
programme.desc: (1971) Sci-fi thriller. When a space rocket crash-lands in the US, a fatal alien virus is released, sparking a desperate battle to defeat the extraterrestrial life form and save mankind.

programme.title: Midway
programme.desc: (2019) Roland Emmerich's sprawling, action-packed war drama about the Battle of Midway, a major turning point in World War II. Starring Ed Skrein, Luke Evans and Woody Harrelson.

programme.title: Battleship
programme.desc: (2012) Sci-fi action-adventure starring Taylor Kitsch, Liam Neeson and Rihanna. When aliens land spacecraft in the Pacific Ocean, only the US and Japanese navies can save Earth.

programme.title: Bad Boys for Life
programme.desc: (2020) Action-comedy starring Will Smith and Martin Lawrence. Just when Marcus is planning to retire, a vengeful drug lord's son shoots Mike. Can our dynamic duo beat the cartel?

programme.title: Alita: Battle Angel
programme.desc: (2019) Partially animated action film based on a popular manga comic. Alita, a deadly cyborg brought back to life by a mourning scientist, sets out to discover her true identity.

programme.title: Small, Slow But Steady
programme.desc: (2022) Poignant drama with Yukino Kishii. An aspiring young boxer - who is also deaf - tries to get her career off the ground during the early days of the Covid pandemic. In Japanese/subs.

programme.desc: This time around, singing cowboy Roy Rogers (Roy Rogers) is an insurance investigator sent to find a stash of money lifted from a company payroll. Together, Roy and Betty set out to prove her father was wrongly accused and track down the real criminal.

programme.title: The Man From Utah
programme.desc: One day, John Weston (John Wayne) helps stop a bank robbery. George Higgins (George Hayes), the town's grateful marshal, asks Weston for his help in solving another crime. It seems someone at a touring rodeo show is killing the top riders before they can take home the trophy -- and the prize money. Weston agrees to investigate and works his way into the show, which is run by the shady Spike Barton (Edward Peil). As Weston gets closer to the truth, he finds himself in even greater danger.

programme.title: Think Big
programme.desc: Children are the future -- and so are their inventions, which is why "Think Big" could be an important show. It features young inventors competing to see who can come up with the most-innovative invention. Each episode features two teams in the Think Tank -- a studio filled with art supplies and construction materials -- who brainstorm and choose materials before sketching, designing and building a prototype to be presented to a judge, who determines a winner. The champions earn bragging rights and the coveted Genius Cup.

programme.title: America's Heartland
programme.desc: Much of the food Americans eat is produced by farmers and ranchers in the country's heartland, but many people don't know how it's produced. "America's Heartland" provides information about the people and processes responsible for the availability of food and fuel across the country and around the world. The show's reporters and producers tell stories in topics that include farm families, consumer issues, animal welfare and crop sustainability. Some episodes also take the show abroad to countries such as Egypt and Taiwan to show the impact American agriculture has on the global economy.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Inspired by the real-life Emergency Task Force of Toronto, Ontario, Canada, this taut police drama chronicles the efforts of the Strategic Response Unit (SRU), an elite and highly skilled group of cops charged with rescuing hostages, defusing bombs and breaking up gangs. programme.title: No Room for Error
